scenario: Discourse work session - audit the Upload contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer correctly identifies Discourse as a Rails forum, maps the directory structure (app/models, app/controllers, app/serializers, app/services, app/jobs with Sidekiq subdirs, lib/, plugins/), places Upload at app/models/upload.rb:5, and names the shared pieces (HasUrl, UploadReference, FileStore, UploadCreator) with file:line — the agent can begin at any of these entry points with small lookups. Minor gap: doesn't point to where avatars/themes/backups concretely reference Upload, but the named pieces are sufficient.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every piece is named with a concrete file:line (app/models/upload.rb:5, lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7, lib/upload_creator.rb:59, lib/discourse.rb:958, lib/upload_security.rb:27); no vague 'the model layer' prose anywhere.

justification.rationale: The answer labels each piece with a brief functional tag ('URL/sha1 extraction', 'storage abstraction', 'polymorphic link table') but never explains WHY the architecture is shaped this way — e.g. why URL resolution is extracted into a shared concern, or why a polymorphic link table was chosen over direct associations on every referencing model.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere; every piece is stated with full confidence, including pieces like OptimizedVideo and UserUpload that were not opened — the answer doesn't tell the agent which associations or concern methods it didn't confirm.

relationship.rationale: The answer does better than a flat file list: it names HasUrl as a concern shared with OptimizedImage, labels UploadReference as the polymorphic link table, and marks HasPostUploadReferences as the consumer side on Post/PostLocalization — but never writes the explicit path 'Post → UploadReference → Upload' or states how avatars/themes reach Upload, leaving the agent to infer the traversal route.

steps.step: Map the Upload contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: Covers every rubric element — HasUrl concern at app/models/concerns/has_url.rb with method-level lines, all associations at upload.rb:17-34, OptimizedImage/OptimizedVideo/TopicThumbnail derived records with file:line, and a 'what to preserve' section on sha1 duality, url derivation, and teardown shape; an agent could safely pick any of these as an entry point without fresh exploration.

specificity.rationale: File:line is provided throughout: upload.rb:5/7/17-34/53-58/60-64/212-217/223, has_url.rb:21/49, optimized_image.rb:35, optimized_video.rb:5/47-56, topic_thumbnail.rb:7/11, upload_creator.rb:170-178, base_store.rb:124-140 — nothing described as 'the model layer' or 'various callbacks'.

justification.rationale: The four 'what a teammate must know' points explain WHY sha1 is overloaded (secure-uploads fake hash), WHY url is not authoritative (store re-derives path via regex), and WHY teardown is hand-rolled (three callbacks + cascade mix); missing is a WHY for pulling URL resolution into a concern rather than inlining it, and WHY variants are separate records rather than columns.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer presents every piece with full confidence and never flags a method it didn't open, an association it inferred, or a line range it couldn't verify — an agent reading it has no signal about where to double-check before acting.

relationship.rationale: Each piece states its connecting mechanism: HasUrl as an included mixin supplying get_from_url/extract_sha1, associations typed as belongs_to/has_many/through/foreign_key/dependent, OptimizedImage spawned via OptimizedImage.create_for, OptimizedVideo owning a second Upload via optimized_upload, and url derivation routed through base_store.rb regex before sha1 fallback — not a bare file list.

steps.step: Trace how an upload is created and processed

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the full path with file:line in numbered order: entry points → UploadCreator inline pipeline (7 steps) → storage → async fan-out (jobs, lazy thumbnails, post-processing, S3 ACL). The inline/async split is labeled explicitly throughout, giving the agent a complete route to follow safely.

specificity.rationale: Every layer is named with concrete file:line — upload_creator.rb:59/91/259, optimized_image.rb:35, cooked_post_processor.rb:143/176/221, app/jobs paths — rather than vague category names. Line ranges are given for the inline pipeline steps.

justification.rationale: The answer labels what is async (Sidekiq, lazy-on-demand) but never explains WHY: there is no statement that video conversion or avatar thumbnail generation is deferred to keep the request fast. The design rationale is absent for every async split.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer notes 'separate from upload creation' for post-processing and 'when on S3' for remote storage, but does not flag any part of the chain it could not confirm or any store-dependent branch the agent should verify before acting.

relationship.rationale: The chain is written as explicit hops: 'both funnel into UploadCreator', 'store_upload nils the url then calls store_file', 'after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job → Jobs::ConvertVideo', post-processing 'triggered when a post referencing the upload is cooked' — each connector is named, not just the endpoints.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract

map_quality.rationale: All eight rubric-named areas are covered and two bonus groups (upload controller surface, FK-holding model surface) are added; every dependent is listed individually with file:line and a use phrase, including the S3 inventory reconciliation, site-setting hydration, backup creator, plugin register-upload callbacks, and email styles — the exact non-obvious scatter the rubric targets.

specificity.rationale: Every item carries a concrete file:line and at least one identifying method or field name — no item collapses into 'various lib helpers'; even rake tasks cite exact task names and line numbers.

justification.rationale: Area groupings carry implicit WHY, and most items include a phrase that explains the functional role ('groups remote uploads by sha1 for archiving', 'feed Upload.add_unused_callback … consumed by CleanUpUploads'); a handful of the mid-list CookedProcessorMixin entries only name what the method does rather than why the Upload contract change forces a review.

uncertainty.rationale: There is no explicit 'what a static pass can miss' caveat — no warning that concern- or association-reached dependents may be incomplete, no flag on dynamic dispatch or plugins outside the discourse-ai/chat bundle; the answer is presented as exhaustive without any hedge.

relationship.rationale: The non-obvious routes are largely stated: Plugin::Instance's callback registration route to Upload is spelled out with upload.rb:96-118, Email::Styles reaches uploads 'by sha', BackupRestore::Creator 'groups remote uploads by sha1', SiteSettingExtension's hydrate_uploads_in_objects is named, Section J explicitly labels the FK/association surface and its teardown callbacks; weaker spots are a few mid-group CookedProcessorMixin entries where the path from the method to Upload is implied but not stated.

steps.step: Trace how upload identity and storage location are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer catalogs guard mechanisms with file:line across three well-organized sections (identity, access/ownership, URL resolvers), giving the agent enough to locate every major piece; however it does not trace how these guards are invoked inside the specific service paths the prompt names (post-processing, email rendering, backup, S3 reconciliation), so the agent still needs targeted follow-ups to connect the catalog to each consumer.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every guard and resolver is named by exact method and file:line — `HasUrl#extract_sha1 :11`, `FileStore::S3Store#has_been_uploaded? lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:142`, `Upload.secure_uploads_url? app/models/upload.rb:248` — leaving almost no vague references to 'the validation code'.

justification.rationale: The answer gives implicit WHY in a few places ('branches on `secure?` (presigned vs cdn url)', 'reconcile DB records against the actual S3 inventory by `etag`') but never states the overarching reason guards are needed across service boundaries — that uploads may be secure, physically missing, or on remote storage — so the agent must infer the motivation rather than read it directly.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags nothing as unconfirmed or potentially missing; it presents all three sections as complete without noting paths that assume the upload resolves without a guard or areas the static pass could not open, leaving the agent to discover gaps on its own.

relationship.rationale: A handful of entries do state the protecting relation ('before acting/removing' for `has_been_uploaded?`, 'Guardian `can_see?`' for `check_secure_upload_permission`) but the chain from a named consumer (email renderer, backup creator, S3-reconciliation job) through a specific guard to the protected action is never assembled; the three sections remain a catalog of components rather than a set of connected paths, so the agent cannot trace which guard gates which downstream effect without re-reading each consumer.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers every area the rubric names — storage/CDN (BaseStore, S3Store, UrlHelper), jobs (CleanUpUploads, SyncAccessControlForUploads), email (styles.rb, receiver.rb), backup (creator.rb), theming/profiles (items 13-14), plugin/site-setting (item 15), and post rendering (item 10) — with file:line throughout and a four-tier risk ranking plus a 'must re-verify' checklist; an agent could pick any layer and start with targeted lookups. Lower tiers (discourse-ai, chat, calendar) are listed without file:line, leaving a small gap.

specificity.rationale: High-risk items carry exact file:line (e.g., `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124,142`, `s3_store.rb:218,232,242`, `app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb:7`, `lib/backup_restore/creator.rb:126,216,346`); medium-tier items name the files but line numbers thin out, and plugin/AI dependents (item 15) carry no file:line at all.

justification.rationale: The answer explains WHY each tier is its rank — URL-regex misfires orphan store keys (item 1), sha1↔url resolver breaks secure-endpoint lookups and PrettyText site-wide (item 2), secure-vs-CDN branching leaks or 403s files (item 3), teardown misses leave dangling FKs (item 4) — with causal chains, not just labels; justification weakens for medium/lower tiers which mostly get one-phrase summaries.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer never explicitly flags what it could not confirm or where the static pass may be incomplete (e.g., concern-reached or dynamically-dispatched dependents); the 'must re-verify' closing section is prescriptive, not uncertainty-flagging, and lower-tier items get 'verify but contained' without naming what specifically might be missed.

relationship.rationale: Most high-risk items state the connecting route explicitly — `HasUrl` concern supplies sha1↔url resolution (item 2), `BaseStore` derives paths from the URL scheme (item 1), `OptimizedImage` is spawned via `dependent: :destroy` cascade (item 9), S3Inventory reconciles via `etag`/`verification_status` fields (item 8) — but medium/lower tiers name files and effects without the relation that makes each one reachable from the Upload contract change.

map_quality.rationale: The map is complete and actionable: files-to-edit with method names and line numbers, all dependents across 10 named areas, 20+ spec files listed, fixtures called out (`spec/support/uploads_helpers.rb:4,27`), plugin suites noted, and a lint instruction — a teammate could land the change from this map alone. The JSON verification_map block reinforces the prose with an explicit edit/specs/migration checklist.

specificity.rationale: Every edit target carries file:line and method names (e.g. `app/models/upload.rb` `destroy` at `:212`, `before_destroy` `:53-64`, `S3Store#url_for :218`); spec files are exact paths, not 'the store specs'; the only minor gap is a few line numbers expressed as `:NN` shorthand rather than `file:NN` form.

justification.rationale: Area groupings carry implicit WHY (email strips secure images, backup bundles remote uploads, FK holders need nulling on destroy), and the `must_reverify` list states reasons. However, within each area many individual files are listed without an explicit sentence explaining what the change does to them — the justification is structural rather than per-file explanatory.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags `(add if missing)` for `optimized_video_spec.rb`, issues a migration-skill warning, and qualifies the touch set as conditional (`if url/path/teardown changes`). It does not, however, explicitly call out areas where it is unsure about connections or potential missed dependents — the answer reads mostly confident throughout.

relationship.rationale: Most edited files are tied to the change via their relationship: HasUrl for URL-resolution behavior, UploadReference as the polymorphic link table, the `before_destroy` callbacks that null FK holders, and the storage chain (BaseStore→S3Store→cdn_url). Dependents in post-rendering name the exact method paths (`link_post_uploads`, `each_upload_url`) and the guards section names the resolver that protects each downstream path — a plain file list this is not.
